operating_state: Net sales increased 2.2% to $44.6 billion driven by 1% pricing increases and 1% favorable foreign exchange, with volume and mix unchanged. Beauty delivered mid-single digit growth from Hair Care and Personal Care innovation while Health Care posted mid-single digit increases led by premium product mix. Fabric & Home Care remained flat while Baby, Feminine & Family Care declined low single digits due to competitive pressures. Operating income declined to $5.4 billion from $5.7 billion due to increased restructuring costs and higher SG&A expenses.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 90 basis points to 51.3% driven by unfavorable product mix, 60 basis points of tariff pressure, and product investments, partially offset by 170 basis points of manufacturing productivity savings. SG&A expenses increased 30 basis points to 26.1% of sales from higher marketing investments, wage inflation, and restructuring spending. Operating margin declined 130 basis points to 25.2% despite productivity initiatives delivering 100 basis points of SG&A benefit.
material_events: Company completed Argentina operations liquidation generating $0.8 billion after-tax restructuring charges and announced comprehensive portfolio plan targeting $1.5-2.0 billion in restructuring costs over two years with up to 7,000 personnel reductions. Executed $3.5 billion in share repurchases and paid $5.1 billion in dividends. Clorox joint venture for Glad products terminates January 2026 with expected $500 million cash proceeds and $250-300 million after-tax gain. Completed debt issuances totaling $2.0 billion USD and €1.0 billion EUR notes.
macro_exposures: Tariff exposure created 60 basis points of gross margin pressure across segments with particular impact on Beauty and Health Care operations. Foreign currency translation provided modest positive impact while emerging market devaluations continue affecting exposures. Commodity price fluctuations in oil-derived materials and paper-based inputs remain elevated with geopolitical tensions maintaining modest consolidated exposure.
forward_outlook: Management expects restructuring program to deliver half of total costs by fiscal 2026 end with remainder in fiscal 2027, targeting improved cost structure through streamlined portfolio and organization. Glad joint venture termination proceeds of approximately $500 million anticipated in Q3 fiscal 2026. Management reaffirmed expectations for approximately $5 billion in direct share repurchases during fiscal year 2026.
uncertainty_profile: Gillette indefinite-lived intangible asset fair value exceeds carrying value by greater than 10%, with $12.8 billion carrying value subject to impairment risk from adverse macroeconomic conditions or currency devaluation. Pending fair market value determination for Glad joint venture creates timing and valuation uncertainty for expected Q3 fiscal 2026 transaction. Company faces ongoing tax audits across multiple jurisdictions with accrued liabilities while restructuring execution risks remain across business units through fiscal 2027.
